chunks.title: Components & Setup
chunks.content:
## Components Per Player

| Color Count | Followers Each | As Scoring Marker | As Play Supply |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 of 5 colors | 8 | 1 (placed on score track) | 7 (kept in front of player) |

**Full component list:** 72 land tiles (1 start tile with distinct back), 1 scoreboard, 40 followers in 5 colors (8 per player).

## Setup Steps

1. Place the **start tile** face-up in the center of the table (distinct back identifies it).
2. Shuffle the remaining **71 tiles** face-down into several stacks within reach of all players.
3. Place the **scoreboard** near the table edge.
4. Each player picks a color and takes **8 followers**.
5. Place **1 follower** on space **0** of the scoreboard as scoring marker.
6. Keep remaining **7 followers** in personal supply in front of you.
7. The player who **most recently visited a walled city** goes first (or determine randomly); play proceeds **clockwise**.

chunks.title: Turn Actions
chunks.content:
## Turn Actions (in order)

On your turn, perform these steps in order:

| Step | Action | Required? | Details |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|
| 1 | Draw & place a tile | **Mandatory** | Draw 1 tile from any face-down stack; place adjacent to existing tiles matching all edges (road↔road, city↔city, field↔field). If unplaceable, discard and draw again. |
| 2 | Place a follower | Optional | Place 1 follower from your supply onto any feature of the tile **just placed**. Cannot place on a feature already occupied by any follower (even via connected tiles). |
| 3 | Score completed features | Mandatory (if applicable) | Check roads, cities, and monasteries for completion; score immediately. Farmers do **not** score mid-game. |

### Follower Placement Roles

| Placement Location | Role |
|--------------------|------|
| Road segment | Thief |
| City segment | Knight |
| Monastery tile | Monk |
| Field segment | Farmer |

**Supply:** Each player has 7 followers available (1 is used as score marker). Followers on completed features are returned to your supply after scoring. Farmers stay on the board until end-game.

chunks.title: Mid-Game Scoring (Feature Completion)
chunks.content:
## Mid-Game Scoring — Completed Features

Triggered in Step 3 of your turn whenever a placed tile completes a feature.

| Feature | Completion Condition | Points |
|---------|----------------------|--------|
| Road | Both ends terminated (crossroads, city, monastery, or loop) | **1 pt per tile** in road |
| City | Fully enclosed (no wall gaps) | **2 pts per tile** + **2 pts per pennant (coat of arms)** |
| Monastery | Surrounded on all 8 sides by tiles | **9 pts** (1 monastery tile + 8 surrounding tiles) |
| Farm | — | Does not score mid-game |

### Shared Feature Majority Rule
- Player with **most followers** on the feature scores **full points**.
- **Tied players each score full points** independently.
- Minority holders score nothing.
- All followers on the scored feature are returned to their owners' supplies.

chunks.title: Tile Types & Placement Rules
chunks.content:
## Tile Types & Placement Rules

All edges must match when placing a tile (road→road, city→city, field→field).

| Tile Type | Key Feature | Placement Note |
|---|---|---|
| City tiles | Enclosed city segments | All city edges must connect to city edges |
| Road tiles | Road segments ending at crossroads, city, monastery, or loop | Both ends must terminate to complete the road |
| Monastery (Cloister) | Single monastery in center of tile | Scores when all 8 surrounding tiles are placed |
| City + Coat of Arms (Pennant) | Shield symbol inside city | Worth extra points (see scoring) |
| Starting tile | City edge on one side, road, open fields | Pre-placed at game start; differently marked back |
| Combination tiles | Mix of city, road, and field | Edges must still match all adjacent tiles |

chunks.title: Follower Roles by Tile Feature
chunks.content:
## Follower Roles by Tile Feature

When placing a follower after tile placement, the feature it occupies determines its role:

| Feature Placed On | Follower Role | Scores When |
|---|---|---|
| Road segment | Thief (Highwayman) | Road is completed (both ends terminated) |
| City segment | Knight | City is fully enclosed |
| Monastery tile | Monk | Monastery tile is surrounded on all 8 sides |
| Field segment | Farmer | End of game only |

**Restriction:** Cannot place on any feature already occupied by any follower (yours or opponent's), even across multiple connected tiles.

chunks.title: Coat of Arms (Pennant) Tiles — Point Value
chunks.content:
## Coat of Arms (Pennant) Tiles — Point Value

Some city tiles contain a shield/coat-of-arms symbol. These modify scoring:

| Situation | Points per Pennant |
|---|---|
| City completed (during game or end-game) | +2 points each |
| City incomplete (end-game only) | +1 point each |

**Note:** Pennant bonuses apply in addition to the per-tile city score. Multiple pennants in one city all count.

chunks.title: Ongoing Scoring (During Play)
chunks.content:
## Ongoing Scoring (During Play)

Score immediately when a feature is **completed** after tile placement.

| Feature | Completion Condition | Points |
|---|---|---|
| Road | Both ends terminate (crossroads, city, monastery, or loops) | 1 pt per tile |
| City | Fully enclosed (no gaps) | 2 pts per tile + 2 pts per pennant |
| Monastery | Surrounded on all 8 sides | 9 pts total (1 + 8 surrounding) |
| Farm | — | Does not score during play |

**Shared features (majority rule):** Player(s) with the most followers score full points. Tied players each score full points. Minority followers return with 0 points.

Followers on scored features are returned to their owner's supply.

chunks.title: End-Game Scoring
chunks.content:
## End-Game Scoring

Triggered immediately when the last tile is placed.

### Incomplete Features

| Feature | Points |
|---|---|
| Incomplete road | 1 pt per tile |
| Incomplete city | 1 pt per tile + 1 pt per pennant (half the in-game rate) |
| Incomplete monastery | 1 pt for monastery tile + 1 pt per surrounding tile (max 9) |

### Farms (Fields)

- Each **completed** city adjacent to a farm scores **3 pts** for the controlling farmer(s).
- Incomplete cities do **not** count.
- Majority rule applies across connected fields: player(s) with the most farmers on a connected field each score full points.

### Majority Rule (all features)

Most followers on a feature = full points. Tied players each score full points.

### Tiebreaker

No official tiebreaker is defined in the base rules — tied scores share the victory.

chunks.title: Strategic Tips for First-Time Players
chunks.content:
## Strategic Tips for First-Time Players

- **Farm early, farm quietly.** Farmers never come back to your hand — but each completed city adjacent to your farm scores 3 points at game end. Sneaking a farmer onto a large field early can be worth more than multiple road/city grabs.
- **Don't over-commit followers to roads.** Roads score only 1 point per tile; cities score 2. Reserve followers for cities and farms unless you're sure the road will close fast.
- **Prioritize pennant (coat-of-arms) cities.** Each pennant doubles the tile's value in a completed city. A 3-tile city with 2 pennants scores 10 points — fight for those.
- **Force shared majorities instead of fighting.** Connecting into an opponent's city or road to tie the lead is often better than starting a new feature — you score full points for free and they gain nothing extra.
- **Don't park a monk unless the monastery is likely to fill.** A monastery needs all 8 surrounding tiles placed. In a 2-player game with fewer tiles on the board, interior monasteries are safer bets than edge ones.
- **Incomplete cities score half — cut your losses.** If a city won't close, sometimes it's better to stop defending it and retrieve nothing (you get the follower back at game end anyway) rather than deepening investment.
- **Watch your supply.** You only have 7 active followers. Locking too many into long-running features leaves you unable to contest new opportunities — keep 2–3 free at all times.
